channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Passenger flight and Air Force jet diverted from potential collision at DC airport
item.description: A U.S. passenger flight preparing to leave the nation's capital and an incoming military jet received instructions to divert and prevent a possible collision, officials said. Delta Air Lines Flight 2983 was cleared for takeoff at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport on Friday around 3:15 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas reaches 400 measles cases as US deals with outbreaks in 5 states
item.description: At least five states have active measles outbreaks as of Friday, and Texas' is the largest with 400 cases.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: UPS launching online tool to show tariff costs to customers
item.description: “Until now, international purchases often arrived with an unpleasant surprise – an additional bill for unpaid import costs.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's election order creates much confusion before the next federal election in 2026
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — President Donald Trump’s executive order seeking to change how U.S. elections are run is creating uncertainty for state and local election officials and worries about voter confusion before the next federal election, the 2026 midterms.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wisconsin judge won't stop Musk's $1 million payments to voters. Attorney general appeals
item.description: A Wisconsin judge has declined to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters in the state at a planned rally days before the closely contested Supreme Court election.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New wave of smaller, cheaper nuclear reactors sends US states racing to attract the industry
item.description: U.S. states are positioning themselves to compete for newer, cheaper nuclear reactors being developed as communities and tech giants compete in a race for electricity.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Oregon city at heart of high court homelessness ruling blocked from enforcing camping ban
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — An Oregon judge issued a preliminary injunction Friday blocking the city at the heart of a U.S. Supreme Court ruling on homeless encampments from enforcing its camping rules unless it meets certain conditions, as part of a lawsuit filed by advocates.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk says xAI buys social media platform X for $45 billion
item.description: Elon Musk said on Friday his artificial intelligence firm xAI has bought his social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, WFLA, NBC

item.title: South Carolina wildfire keeps growing as firefighters protect homes
item.description: A large wildfire in the South Carolina mountains has doubled in size on each of the last three days. But fire crews have been able to keep the blaze away from structures.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Voice of America wins in court, for now, as judge blocks Trump administration from firing staff
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Voice of America can’t be silenced just yet. A federal judge on Friday halted the Trump administration’s efforts to dismantle the eight-decade-old U.S. government-funded international news service, calling the move a “classic case of arbitrary and capricious decision making.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US Naval Academy ends affirmative action in admissions
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — The U.S. Naval Academy will no longer consider race, ethnicity or sex as a factor for admission to the service institution, a response to an executive order by President Donald Trump, according to federal court documents made public Friday.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: California Pizza Kitchen celebrates 40 years with throwback menu and prizes
item.description: California Pizza Kitchen is celebrating its 40th anniversary with a series of promotions, returning menu items and a collaboration with actor and former employee actress Busy Philipps.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: The top-rated seafood restaurant in every state, according to Yelp
item.description: There are plenty of fish in the sea, as the old saying goes. Unfortunately, there are far fewer exceptional chefs who can cook one for dinner.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Consumer sentiment falls to lowest level in over two years
item.description: The University of Michigan's Survey of Consumers found a nearly 12% drop in consumer sentiment to 57 in March.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Son's skeletal remains found in home of man who died in scuba accident days earlier: report
item.description: A Georgia man died in a scuba diving accident merely days before the skeletal remains of his son, who had been missing for four years, were found in his backyard.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Truck hauling over 125 cows rolls over in Northwest Indiana
item.description: LAKE COUNTY, Ind. — Two cows were injured after a truck hauling dairy cattle overturned in northwest Indiana during rush hour on Friday morning. According to Officials from the Gary Fire Department, the crash unfolded just before 8 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, NewsNation

item.title: Waterbury woman pleads not guilty to holding stepson captive for decades
item.description: WATERBURY, Conn. (WTNH) — The Waterbury woman accused of holding her stepson captive for several decades pleaded not guilty in Waterbury Superior Court on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration moves to end union rights for many federal workers
item.description: The order targets agencies it says have a national security mission but many of the departments don’t have a strict national security connection.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 150
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University is latest Palestinian supporter swept up in US crackdown
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Turkish student detained by federal officers as she walked along a street in a Boston suburb is the latest supporter of Palestinian causes to be swept up in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who express their political views.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Another federal judge blocks Trump policy banning transgender troops in the military
item.description: TACOMA, Wash. (AP) — A U.S. judge in Washington state has blocked enforcement of President Donald Trump’s order banning transgender people from serving in the military, the second nationwide injunction against the policy in as many weeks. The order Thursday from U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mixed verdict for 3 Philadelphia detectives in perjury trial involving a 2016 murder exoneration
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The trial of three long-retired Philadelphia detectives accused of lying about evidence in a homicide case that later ended with an exoneration wrapped up with a mixed verdict Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Here are the international students and faculty known to be targeted by ICE
item.description: The Trump administration’s crackdown on foreign students and faculty who have voiced support for the Palestinian cause has escalated, with universities across the country seeing students either arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or having their visas revoked.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Sundance Film Festival is leaving Utah for Colorado, starting in 2027
item.description: Boulder has officially been unveiled as the city to host the Sundance Film Festival starting in 2027, according to a press release from the Sundance Institute.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, KRON4

item.title: Judge: High heat in Texas prisons unconstitutional
item.description: A federal judge found the extreme heat in Texas prisons is “plainly unconstitutional” but did not order the state to begin installing air conditioners.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Asian Americans and Pacific Islanders are still targets of hatred 5 years after pandemic surge
item.description: Up until 2020, Anna Wong had gone her entire adulthood in Los Angeles without ever facing blatantly racist abuse for being Asian. After COVID-19 hit, she was accosted twice in six months.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justice Department goes to bat for Trump in hush money case
item.description: The Justice Department went to bat for President Trump in his hush money criminal case Wednesday, urging for the case to be moved so he can attempt to toss his guilty verdict on presidential immunity grounds.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: NTSB chairwoman says reviewing the data after midair crash may prevent the next aviation accident
item.description: Someone should have spotted the alarming number of near misses in the skies over the nation's capital before the fatal midair collision that killed 67 people in January, and reviewing the data now could prevent future crashes, according to the head of the agency investigating the crash.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NPR, PBS facing DOGE subcommittee over alleged liberal lean
item.description: At stake are potential cost cuts for both companies, which are partially funded by the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden’s ‘ghost guns’ crackdown
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld the Biden administration’s regulation of “ghost guns” in a 7-2 decision Wednesday, rejecting a challenge brought by gun rights groups and several manufacturers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, ABC 4

item.title: How the Signal 'group chat' furor has affected the 5 biggest players
item.description: The fact that such inherently sensitive information was being communicated in such a forum — and in front of a reporter whom no other participant appears to have noticed — has caused a political storm.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, Fox 5

item.title: Amanda Knox: Prosecutor who put me in prison still texts me every day
item.description: Amanda Knox, convicted, then acquitted of murder, discusses her time in an Italian prison and what freedom means to her now.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, Fox 5

item.title: Mia Love's family announces funeral plans
item.description: The family of Mia Love has announced funeral plans for the former Utah congresswoman, who passed away Sunday, March 23, at the age of 49, after battling brain cancer.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Iowa law banning school library books that depict sex acts on hold again after a new federal ruling
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa cannot, for now, continue to enforce part of its book ban law, a federal judge said Tuesday, giving major publishers that sued the state the second temporary reprieve they requested. The new decision from U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gunman who killed 23 in racist attack at Texas Walmart is offered plea deal to avoid death penalty
item.description: The gunman who killed 23 people in a racist attack targeting Hispanic shoppers at a Walmart near the U.S.-Mexico border in 2019 would avoid the death penalty under a plea offer announced Tuesday, abruptly ending years of efforts by prosecutors to see that he face execution by lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A new Trump portrait for Colorado's Capitol could take time after one he disliked was removed
item.description: Filling an empty space on the wall of presidential portraits in the Colorado Capitol with a new painting of Donald Trump could take time after one he disliked was removed and put into storage.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge orders a June trial for US government's felony case against Boeing
item.description: A federal judge in Texas has set a June trial date for the U.S. government's years-old conspiracy case against Boeing for misleading regulators about the 737 Max jetliner before two of the planes crashed, killing 346 people. U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
